included: DB cache and discovery adapter proof

claimBoundary.nonCommunityPolicy: This roadmap compares engineering and product capability only; GitHub stars, downloads, contributor count, brand awareness, and community size remain out of scope.
claimBoundary.transportParityPolicy: Do not claim Kitex or gRPC-Go transport parity until stream, resolver, balancer, connection lifecycle, retry, deadline, benchmark, and rollback evidence are all promoted from candidate to blocking gates.
claimBoundary.migrationProofPolicy: Migration claims must name a runnable example, a smoke command, compatibility caveats, rollback action, support bundle evidence, and an owner gate before appearing in adopter docs.
claimBoundary.performanceClaimPolicy: Latency remains report-only until multi-run trend confidence promotes a row; allocation budgets may block only through the benchmark ratchet contract.

dimensions.gap: After R6, the remaining non-community gap is converting adoption confidence into deeper runnable evidence instead of adding new high-level claims.
dimensions.todo: Publish the R7 roadmap and boundary inventory as the active aiflow batch with ordered task ids, acceptance gates, ignored runtime paths, and claim boundaries.

dimensions.gap: Top frameworks are easier to adopt because migration paths are immediately runnable. gofly has decision evidence, but each migration path should carry stronger smoke, rollback, and failure-report evidence.
dimensions.todo: Upgrade Gin, go-zero, Kratos, and Kitex migration paths with runnable smoke rows, support bundle handoff, rollback trigger, and compatibility caveat evidence.

dimensions.gap: Kitex and gRPC-Go still have deeper transport and streaming maturity. gofly should turn Tier 1 promotion blockers into explicit stream, resolver, balancer, retry, deadline, auth, tracing, benchmark, and rollback rows.
dimensions.todo: Add RPC Tier 1 promotion evidence rows that separate supported behavior, candidate behavior, report-only latency, and rollback triggers.

dimensions.gap: goctl and Kratos scaffolds are trusted because imports, aliases, historical fixtures, and regeneration diffs are predictable. gofly should deepen importer and profile compatibility evidence.
dimensions.todo: Add importer edge-case compatibility rows for API imports, proto imports, alias collisions, generated-only dependencies, repeat generation diffs, and rollback.

dimensions.gap: Production frameworks make SQL, Redis, cache, and discovery adapters feel owned. gofly has ownership evidence, but needs deeper adapter proof rows tied to smoke commands and fallback behavior.
dimensions.todo: Add DB, cache, and discovery proof rows for supported adapters, generated-project boundaries, dependency triggers, observability, fallback behavior, and rollback.

dimensions.gap: Plugin and template publishing still needs stronger supply-chain trust than example-level adoption. gofly should bind manifest, digest, permission, compatibility, remote template, and failure isolation evidence together.
dimensions.todo: Add plugin and template supply-chain rows covering registry schema, manifest, digest, permission review, compatibility window, malicious paths, and no partial writes.

dimensions.gap: Mature frameworks make release artifacts and CI gates predictable. gofly has release evidence, but tag release, artifact manifest, SBOM, checksum, Trivy, provenance, and required-check drift should remain tied to adopter decisions.
dimensions.todo: Tighten release CI supply-chain enforcement evidence with artifact producer, required-check, security scan, provenance, rollback, and publish/block decision rows.

dimensions.gap: Top HTTP and RPC frameworks retain stronger performance credibility. gofly should keep latency conservative while making multi-run trend confidence and allocation budget promotion explicit.
dimensions.todo: Add performance trend promotion evidence for multi-run confidence, promoted allocation budgets, report-only latency rows, unsupported surfaces, and adopter actions.

dimensions.gap: Mature frameworks help adopters recover when things fail. gofly has doctor and support bundles, but should link remediation next actions back to migration, release, generated project, and dashboard evidence.
dimensions.todo: Add a remediation loop contract tying doctor JSON, release check JSON, support bundle redaction, generated failure reports, dashboard fields, and next actions.

dimensions.gap: Every R7 task must end with commit evidence, gate evidence, known risks, runtime-state exclusions, and aiflow completion so the new workflow stays auditable.
dimensions.todo: Record R7 convergence with per-task commit ids, executed gates, skipped or downgraded checks, runtime ignored paths, and next-round recommendations.
